verses.text: Betrayed and hunted, David prays, 'Save me, O God, by thy name,' against strangers who have risen up and do not set God before them. He pleads God's own name. When men forget God, the believer appeals to the God they ignore.

Betrayed and hunted, David prays, 'Save me, O God, by thy name,' against strangers who have risen up and do not set God before them. He pleads God's own name. When men forget God, the believer appeals to the God they ignore.

Betrayed and hunted, David prays, 'Save me, O God, by thy name,' against strangers who have risen up and do not set God before them. He pleads God's own name. When men forget God, the believer appeals to the God they ignore.
'Behold, God is mine helper,' he declares, sure that God will requite the evil of his foes. Confidence rises mid-trouble. To know God is our helper turns trembling prayer into settled faith.
'Behold, God is mine helper,' he declares, sure that God will requite the evil of his foes. Confidence rises mid-trouble. To know God is our helper turns trembling prayer into settled faith.

He vows a freewill offering, praising God's name, for 'he hath delivered me out of all trouble.' Deliverance, even foreseen by faith, brings thanksgiving. The believer praises before the rescue is fully felt.

He vows a freewill offering, praising God's name, for 'he hath delivered me out of all trouble.' Deliverance, even foreseen by faith, brings thanksgiving. The believer praises before the rescue is fully felt.
